title: Sioux City Public Museum

infobox.value: is a museum about the history and culture of [Sioux City, Iowa](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa"). Displays include a video about the city's history, Native American artifacts, pioneer artifacts, historic transportation vehicles, agriculture, industry, geology and fossils.

[ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-931599-03-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-931599-03-0 "Special:BookSources/978-1-931599-03-0").

[ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-0-7627-4437-4](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-7627-4437-4 "Special:BookSources/978-0-7627-4437-4").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

History museum in Sioux City, Iowa, United States

Sioux City Public Museum

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Map | |
| Established | 1960; moved location in 2011 |
| Location | 607 4th Street, [Sioux City, Iowa](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa") |
| [Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system") | [42°29′42″N 96°24′11″W﻿ / ﻿42.495006°N 96.403043°W﻿ / 42.495006; -96.403043](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Sioux_City_Public_Museum&params=42.495006_N_96.403043_W_type:landmark) |
| Type | History |
| Website | <http://www.siouxcitymuseum.org/> |

The **Sioux City Public Museum** is a museum about the history and culture of [Sioux City, Iowa](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa"). Displays include a video about the city's history, Native American artifacts, pioneer artifacts, historic transportation vehicles, agriculture, industry, geology and fossils.

The Sioux City Public Museum also operates the [Sergeant Floyd River Museum & Welcome Center](/wiki/Sergeant_Floyd_(towboat) "Sergeant Floyd (towboat)") and the [Peirce Mansion](/wiki/Peirce_Mansion "Peirce Mansion") in Sioux City.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/19/Peirce_Mansion_from_SE_1.jpg/250px-Peirce_Mansion_from_SE_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Peirce_Mansion_from_SE_1.jpg)

Peirce Mansion from SE

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sioux_City_Public_Museum&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

From its opening in 1960 until 2011, the museum was located in the 1893 [Peirce Mansion](/wiki/Peirce_Mansion "Peirce Mansion") at 2901 Jackson Street. First purchased by a group called the Junior League in 1959, the opening of the mansion as a museum was in September 1960.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Exhibits focused on the city's history and there were also exhibits detailing the [Lakota people](/wiki/Lakota_people "Lakota people"), [Omaha people](/wiki/Omaha_people "Omaha people"), and [Winnebago people](/wiki/Winnebago_(tribe) "Winnebago (tribe)").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Subjects in the museum included [anthropology](/wiki/Anthropology "Anthropology"), [archeology](/wiki/Archeology "Archeology"), [natural history](/wiki/Natural_history "Natural history"), [science](/wiki/Science "Science"), and the [military](/wiki/Military "Military").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

There was a robbery of a saddlebag from the museum in 1996 that was part of a string of museum thefts which involved stealing Native American artifacts.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

A new location was opened in 2011 in Sioux City's downtown at a former [J. C. Penney](/wiki/J._C._Penney "J. C. Penney") store which includes a theater, classrooms, and exhibition rooms.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The museum project cost $12.5 million. A new exhibit was opened that is all about the song "[Sioux City Sue](/wiki/Sioux_City_Sue "Sioux City Sue")".[[6]](#cite_note-6) The museum takes up 10,000 [square feet](/wiki/Square_feet "Square feet").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

The Peirce Mansion is still owned by the Museum. It is open to the public for quarterly open house events and is available for rental.

## Activities and recognition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sioux_City_Public_Museum&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Activities and recognition")]

Students from the Sioux City Community School District make models of local landmarks that are judged every year by the museum and the Historical Association.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

The museum's [deaccession](/wiki/Collection_(museum) "Collection (museum)") criteria is a six step process that was used as an example in the book *Museum administration: an introduction*.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
